verses.kjv: And because of all this we make a sure covenant, and write it; and our princes, Levites, and priests, seal unto it.
verses.bsb: In view of all this, we make a binding agreement, putting it in writing and sealing it with the names of our leaders, Levites, and priests.”

verses.kjv: NOW those that sealed were, Nehemiah, the Tirshatha, the son of Hachaliah, and Zidkijah,
verses.bsb: Now these were the ones who sealed the document:  Nehemiah the governor, son of Hacaliah,   and also Zedekiah,

verses.kjv: Seraiah, Azariah, Jeremiah,
verses.bsb: Seraiah, Azariah, Jeremiah,

verses.kjv: Pashur, Amariah, Malchijah,
verses.bsb: Pashhur, Amariah, Malchijah,

verses.kjv: Hattush, Shebaniah, Malluch,
verses.bsb: Hattush, Shebaniah, Malluch,

verses.kjv: Harim, Meremoth, Obadiah,
verses.bsb: Harim, Meremoth, Obadiah,

verses.kjv: Daniel, Ginnethon, Baruch,
verses.bsb: Daniel, Ginnethon, Baruch,

verses.kjv: Meshullam, Abijah, Mijamin,
verses.bsb: Meshullam, Abijah, Mijamin,

verses.kjv: Maaziah, Bilgai, Shemaiah: these were the priests.
verses.bsb: Maaziah, Bilgai, and Shemaiah.   These were the priests.

verses.kjv: And the Levites: both Jeshua the son of Azaniah, Binnui of the sons of Henadad, Kadmiel;
verses.bsb: The Levites:  Jeshua son of Azaniah,   Binnui of the sons of Henadad, Kadmiel,

verses.kjv: And their brethren, Shebaniah, Hodijah, Kelita, Pelaiah, Hanan,
verses.bsb: and their associates: Shebaniah, Hodiah, Kelita, Pelaiah, Hanan,

verses.kjv: Micha, Rehob, Hashabiah,
verses.bsb: Mica, Rehob, Hashabiah,

verses.kjv: Zaccur, Sherebiah, Shebaniah,
verses.bsb: Zaccur, Sherebiah, Shebaniah,

verses.kjv: Hodijah, Bani, Beninu.
verses.bsb: Hodiah, Bani, and Beninu.

verses.kjv: The chief of the people; Parosh, Pahath-moab, Elam, Zatthu, Bani,
verses.bsb: And the leaders of the people:  Parosh, Pahath-moab, Elam, Zattu, Bani,

verses.kjv: Bunni, Azgad, Bebai,
verses.bsb: Bunni, Azgad, Bebai,

verses.kjv: Adonijah, Bigvai, Adin,
verses.bsb: Adonijah, Bigvai, Adin,

verses.kjv: Ater, Hizkijah, Azzur,
verses.bsb: Ater, Hezekiah, Azzur,

verses.kjv: Hodijah, Hashum, Bezai,
verses.bsb: Hodiah, Hashum, Bezai,

verses.kjv: Hariph, Anathoth, Nebai,
verses.bsb: Hariph, Anathoth, Nebai,

verses.kjv: Magpiash, Meshullam, Hezir,
verses.bsb: Magpiash, Meshullam, Hezir,

verses.kjv: Meshezabeel, Zadok, Jaddua,
verses.bsb: Meshezabel, Zadok, Jaddua,

verses.kjv: Pelatiah, Hanan, Anaiah,
verses.bsb: Pelatiah, Hanan, Anaiah,

verses.kjv: Hoshea, Hananiah, Hashub,
verses.bsb: Hoshea, Hananiah, Hasshub,

verses.kjv: Hallohesh, Pileha, Shobek,
verses.bsb: Hallohesh, Pilha, Shobek,

verses.kjv: Rehum, Hashabnah, Maaseiah,
verses.bsb: Rehum, Hashabnah, Maaseiah,

verses.kjv: And Ahijah, Hanan, Anan,
verses.bsb: Ahijah, Hanan, Anan,

verses.kjv: Malluch, Harim, Baanah.
verses.bsb: Malluch, Harim, and Baanah.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the rest of the people, the priests, the Levites, the porters, the singers, the Nethinims, and all they that had separated themselves from the people of the lands unto the law of God, their wives, their sons, and their daughters, every one having knowledge, and having understanding;
verses.bsb: “The rest of the people—the priests, Levites, gatekeepers, singers, temple servants, and all who had separated themselves from the people of the land to obey the Law of God—along with their wives and all their sons and daughters who are able to understand,

verses.kjv: They clave to their brethren, their nobles, and entered into a curse, and into an oath, to walk in God’s law, which was given by Moses the servant of God, and to observe and do all the commandments of the Lord our Lord, and his judgments and his statutes;
verses.bsb: hereby join with their noble brothers and commit themselves with a sworn oath to follow the Law of God given through His servant Moses and to carefully obey all the commandments, ordinances, and statutes of the LORD our Lord.

verses.kjv: And that we would not give our daughters unto the people of the land, nor take their daughters for our sons:
verses.bsb: We will not give our daughters in marriage to the people of the land, and we will not take their daughters for our sons.

verses.kjv: And if the people of the land bring ware or any victuals on the sabbath day to sell, that we would not buy it of them on the sabbath, or on the holy day: and that we would leave the seventh year, and the exaction of every debt.
verses.bsb: When the people of the land bring merchandise or any kind of grain to sell on the Sabbath day, we will not buy from them on a Sabbath or holy day. Every seventh year we will let the fields lie fallow, and will cancel every debt.

verses.kjv: Also we made ordinances for us, to charge ourselves yearly with the third part of a shekel for the service of the house of our God;
verses.bsb: We also place ourselves under the obligation to contribute a third of a shekel yearly for the service of the house of our God:

verses.kjv: For the shewbread, and for the continual meat offering, and for the continual burnt offering, of the sabbaths, of the new moons, for the set feasts, and for the holy things, and for the sin offerings to make an atonement for Israel, and for all the work of the house of our God.
verses.bsb: for the showbread, for the regular grain offerings and burnt offerings, for the Sabbath offerings, for the New Moons and appointed feasts, for the holy offerings, for the sin offerings to make atonement for Israel, and for all the duties of the house of our God.

verses.kjv: And we cast the lots among the priests, the Levites, and the people, for the wood offering, to bring it into the house of our God, after the houses of our fathers, at times appointed year by year, to burn upon the altar of the Lord our God, as it is written in the law:
verses.bsb: We have cast lots among the priests, Levites, and people for the donation of wood by our families at the appointed times each year. They are to bring it to the house of our God to burn on the altar of the LORD our God, as it is written in the Law.

verses.kjv: And to bring the firstfruits of our ground, and the firstfruits of all fruit of all trees, year by year, unto the house of the Lord:
verses.bsb: We will also bring the firstfruits of our land and of every fruit tree to the house of the LORD year by year.

verses.kjv: Also the firstborn of our sons, and of our cattle, as it is written in the law, and the firstlings of our herds and of our flocks, to bring to the house of our God, unto the priests that minister in the house of our God:
verses.bsb: And we will bring the firstborn of our sons and our livestock, as it is written in the Law, and will bring the firstborn of our herds and flocks to the house of our God, to the priests who minister in the house of our God.

verses.kjv: And that we should bring the firstfruits of our dough, and our offerings, and the fruit of all manner of trees, of wine and of oil, unto the priests, to the chambers of the house of our God; and the tithes of our ground unto the Levites, that the same Levites might have the tithes in all the cities of our tillage.
verses.bsb: Moreover, we will bring to the priests at the storerooms of the house of our God the firstfruits of our dough, of our grain offerings, of the fruit of all our trees, and of our new wine and oil. A tenth of our produce belongs to the Levites, so that they shall receive tithes in all the towns where we labor.

verses.kjv: And the priest the son of Aaron shall be with the Levites, when the Levites take tithes: and the Levites shall bring up the tithe of the tithes unto the house of our God, to the chambers, into the treasure house.
verses.bsb: A priest of Aaron’s line is to accompany the Levites when they collect the tenth, and the Levites are to bring a tenth of these tithes to the storerooms of the treasury in the house of our God.

verses.kjv: For the children of Israel and the children of Levi shall bring the offering of the corn, of the new wine, and the oil, unto the chambers, where are the vessels of the sanctuary, and the priests that minister, and the porters, and the singers: and we will not forsake the house of our God.
verses.bsb: For the Israelites and the Levites are to bring the contributions of grain, new wine, and oil to the storerooms where the articles of the sanctuary are kept and where the ministering priests, the gatekeepers, and the singers stay.  Thus we will not neglect the house of our God.”

verses.kjv: AND the rulers of the people dwelt at Jerusalem: the rest of the people also cast lots, to bring one of ten to dwell in Jerusalem the holy city, and nine parts to dwell in other cities.
verses.bsb: Now the leaders of the people settled in Jerusalem, and the rest of the people cast lots to bring one out of ten to live in the holy city of Jerusalem, while the remaining nine were to dwell in their own towns.

verses.kjv: And the people blessed all the men, that willingly offered themselves to dwell at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: And the people blessed all the men who volunteered to live in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now these are the chief of the province that dwelt in Jerusalem: but in the cities of Judah dwelt every one in his possession in their cities, to wit, Israel, the priests, and the Levites, and the Nethinims, and the children of Solomon’s servants.
verses.bsb: These are the heads of the provinces who settled in Jerusalem. (In the villages of Judah, however, each lived on his own property in their towns—the Israelites, priests, Levites, temple servants, and descendants of Solomon’s servants—

verses.kjv: And at Jerusalem dwelt certain of the children of Judah, and of the children of Benjamin. Of the children of Judah; Athaiah the son of Uzziah, the son of Zechariah, the son of Amariah, the son of Shephatiah, the son of Mahalaleel, of the children of Perez;
verses.bsb: while some of the descendants of Judah and Benjamin settled in Jerusalem.)  From the descendants of Judah:  Athaiah son of Uzziah, the son of Zechariah, the son of Amariah, the son of Shephatiah, the son of Mahalalel, a descendant of Perez;

verses.kjv: And Maaseiah the son of Baruch, the son of Col-hozeh, the son of Hazaiah, the son of Adaiah, the son of Joiarib, the son of Zechariah, the son of Shiloni.
verses.bsb: and Maaseiah son of Baruch, the son of Col-hozeh, the son of Hazaiah, the son of Adaiah, the son of Joiarib, the son of Zechariah, a descendant of Shelah.

verses.kjv: All the sons of Perez that dwelt at Jerusalem were four hundred threescore and eight valiant men.
verses.bsb: The descendants of Perez who settled in Jerusalem totaled 468 men of valor.

verses.kjv: And these are the sons of Benjamin; Sallu the son of Meshullam, the son of Joed, the son of Pedaiah, the son of Kolaiah, the son of Maaseiah, the son of Ithiel, the son of Jesaiah.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Benjamin:  Sallu son of Meshullam, the son of Joed, the son of Pedaiah, the son of Kolaiah, the son of Maaseiah, the son of Ithiel, the son of Jeshaiah;

verses.kjv: And after him Gabbai, Sallai, nine hundred twenty and eight.
verses.bsb: and his followers Gabbai and Sallai—928 men.

verses.kjv: And Joel the son of Zichri was their overseer: and Judah the son of Senuah was second over the city.
verses.bsb: Joel son of Zichri was the officer over them, and Judah son of Hassenuah was over the Second District of the city.

verses.kjv: Of the priests: Jedaiah the son of Joiarib, Jachin.
verses.bsb: From the priests:

verses.kjv: Seraiah the son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub, was the ruler of the house of God.
verses.bsb: Jedaiah son of Joiarib; Jachin; Seraiah son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub, the chief official of the house of God;

verses.kjv: And their brethren that did the work of the house were eight hundred twenty and two: and Adaiah the son of Jeroham, the son of Pelaliah, the son of Amzi, the son of Zechariah, the son of Pashur, the son of Malchiah,
verses.bsb: and their associates who did the work at the temple—822 men;

verses.kjv: And his brethren, chief of the fathers, two hundred forty and two: and Amashai the son of Azareel, the son of Ahasai, the son of Meshillemoth, the son of Immer,
verses.bsb: Adaiah son of Jeroham, the son of Pelaliah, the son of Amzi, the son of Zechariah, the son of Pashhur, the son of Malchijah; and his associates, the leaders of families—242 men;

verses.kjv: And their brethren, mighty men of valour, an hundred twenty and eight: and their overseer was Zabdiel, the son of one of the great men.
verses.bsb: Amashai son of Azarel, the son of Ahzai, the son of Meshillemoth, the son of Immer; and his associates—128 mighty men of valor. Zabdiel son of Haggedolim was their overseer.

verses.kjv: Also of the Levites: Shemaiah the son of Hashub, the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Bunni;
verses.bsb: From the Levites:  Shemaiah son of Hasshub, the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Bunni;

verses.kjv: And Shabbethai and Jozabad, of the chief of the Levites, had the oversight of the outward business of the house of God.
verses.bsb: Shabbethai and Jozabad, two leaders of the Levites, who supervised the work outside the house of God;

verses.kjv: And Mattaniah the son of Micha, the son of Zabdi, the son of Asaph, was the principal to begin the thanksgiving in prayer: and Bakbukiah the second among his brethren, and Abda the son of Shammua, the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun.
verses.bsb: Mattaniah son of Mica, the son of Zabdi, the son of Asaph, who led in thanksgiving and prayer; Bakbukiah, second among his associates; and Abda son of Shammua, the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun.

verses.kjv: All the Levites in the holy city were two hundred fourscore and four.
verses.bsb: The Levites in the holy city totaled 284.

verses.kjv: Moreover the porters, Akkub, Talmon, and their brethren that kept the gates, were an hundred seventy and two.
verses.bsb: And the gatekeepers:  Akkub, Talmon, and their associates, who kept watch at the gates—172 men.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the residue of Israel, of the priests, and the Levites, were in all the cities of Judah, every one in his inheritance.
verses.bsb: The rest of the Israelites, with the priests and Levites, were in all the villages of Judah, each on his own inheritance.

verses.kjv: But the Nethinims dwelt in Ophel: and Ziha and Gispa were over the Nethinims.
verses.bsb: The temple servants lived on the hill of Ophel, with Ziha and Gishpa over them.

verses.kjv: The overseer also of the Levites at Jerusalem was Uzzi the son of Bani, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Micha. Of the sons of Asaph, the singers were over the business of the house of God.
verses.bsb: Now the overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem was Uzzi son of Bani, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Mica. He was one of Asaph’s descendants, who were the singers in charge of the service of the house of God.

verses.kjv: For it was the king’s commandment concerning them, that a certain portion should be for the singers, due for every day.
verses.bsb: For there was a command from the king concerning the singers, an ordinance regulating their daily activities.

verses.kjv: And Pethahiah the son of Meshezabeel, of the children of Zerah the son of Judah, was at the king’s hand in all matters concerning the people.
verses.bsb: Pethahiah son of Meshezabel, a descendant of Zerah son of Judah, was the king’s agent in every matter concerning the people.

verses.kjv: And for the villages, with their fields, some of the children of Judah dwelt at Kirjath-arba, and in the villages thereof, and at Dibon, and in the villages thereof, and at Jekabzeel, and in the villages thereof,
verses.bsb: As for the villages with their fields, some of the people of Judah lived in Kiriath-arba, Dibon, Jekabzeel, and their villages;

verses.kjv: And at Jeshua, and at Moladah, and at Beth-phelet,
verses.bsb: in Jeshua, Moladah, and Beth-pelet;

verses.kjv: And at Hazar-shual, and at Beer-sheba, and in the villages thereof,
verses.bsb: in Hazar-shual; in Beersheba and its villages;

verses.kjv: And at Ziklag, and at Mekonah, and in the villages thereof,
verses.bsb: in Ziklag; in Meconah and its villages;

verses.kjv: And at En-rimmon, and at Zareah, and at Jarmuth,
verses.bsb: in En-rimmon, Zorah, Jarmuth,

verses.kjv: Zanoah, Adullam, and in their villages, at Lachish, and the fields thereof, at Azekah, and in the villages thereof. And they dwelt from Beer-sheba unto the valley of Hinnom.
verses.bsb: Zanoah, Adullam, and their villages; in Lachish and its fields; and in Azekah and its villages. So they settled from Beersheba all the way to the Valley of Hinnom.

verses.kjv: The children also of Benjamin from Geba dwelt at Michmash, and Aija, and Beth-el, and in their villages,
verses.bsb: The descendants of Benjamin from Geba lived in Michmash, Aija, and Bethel with its villages;

verses.kjv: And at Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,
verses.bsb: in Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,

verses.kjv: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,
verses.bsb: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,

verses.kjv: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,
verses.bsb: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,

verses.kjv: Lod, and Ono, the valley of craftsmen.
verses.bsb: Lod, and Ono; and in the Valley of the Craftsmen.

verses.kjv: And of the Levites were divisions in Judah, and in Benjamin.
verses.bsb: And some divisions of the Levites of Judah settled in Benjamin.

verses.kjv: NOW these are the priests and the Levites that went up with Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,
verses.bsb: Now these are the priests and Levites who went up with Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and with Jeshua:  Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,

verses.kjv: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,
verses.bsb: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,

verses.kjv: Shechaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,
verses.bsb: Shecaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,

verses.kjv: Iddo, Ginnetho, Abijah,
verses.bsb: Iddo, Ginnethon, Abijah,

verses.kjv: Miamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,
verses.bsb: Mijamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,

verses.kjv: Shemaiah, and Joiarib, Jedaiah,
verses.bsb: Shemaiah, Joiarib, Jedaiah,

verses.kjv: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah. These were the chief of the priests and of their brethren in the days of Jeshua.
verses.bsb: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, and Jedaiah.   These were the leaders of the priests and their associates in the days of Jeshua.

verses.kjv: Moreover the Levites: Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, and Mattaniah, which was over the thanksgiving, he and his brethren.
verses.bsb: The Levites were Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, and Mattaniah, who, with his associates, led the songs of thanksgiving.

verses.kjv: Also Bakbukiah and Unni, their brethren, were over against them in the watches.
verses.bsb: Bakbukiah and Unni, their associates, stood across from them in the services.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Jeshua begat Joiakim, Joiakim also begat Eliashib, and Eliashib begat Joiada,
verses.bsb: Jeshua was the father of Joiakim,   Joiakim was the father of Eliashib,   Eliashib was the father of Joiada,

verses.kjv: And Joiada begat Jonathan, and Jonathan begat Jaddua.
verses.bsb: Joiada was the father of Jonathan,   and Jonathan was the father of Jaddua.

verses.kjv: And in the days of Joiakim were priests, the chief of the fathers: of Seraiah, Meraiah; of Jeremiah, Hananiah;
verses.bsb: In the days of Joiakim, these were the heads of the priestly families:  of the family of Seraiah, Meraiah;   of Jeremiah, Hananiah;

verses.kjv: Of Ezra, Meshullam; of Amariah, Jehohanan;
verses.bsb: of Ezra, Meshullam;   of Amariah, Jehohanan;

verses.kjv: Of Melicu, Jonathan; of Shebaniah, Joseph;
verses.bsb: of Malluchi, Jonathan;   of Shebaniah, Joseph;

verses.kjv: Of Harim, Adna; of Meraioth, Helkai;
verses.bsb: of Harim, Adna;   of Meraioth, Helkai;

verses.kjv: Of Iddo, Zechariah; of Ginnethon, Meshullam;
verses.bsb: of Iddo, Zechariah;   of Ginnethon, Meshullam;

verses.kjv: Of Abijah, Zichri; of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai;
verses.bsb: of Abijah, Zichri;   of Miniamin and of Moadiah, Piltai;

verses.kjv: Of Bilgah, Shammua; of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;
verses.bsb: of Bilgah, Shammua;   of Shemaiah, Jonathan;

verses.kjv: And of Joiarib, Mattenai; of Jedaiah, Uzzi;
verses.bsb: of Joiarib, Mattenai;   of Jedaiah, Uzzi;

verses.kjv: Of Sallai, Kallai; of Amok, Eber;
verses.bsb: of Sallai, Kallai;   of Amok, Eber;

verses.kjv: Of Hilkiah, Hashabiah; of Jedaiah, Nethaneel.
verses.bsb: of Hilkiah, Hashabiah;   and of Jedaiah, Nethanel.

verses.kjv: ¶ The Levites in the days of Eliashib, Joiada, and Johanan, and Jaddua, were recorded chief of the fathers: also the priests, to the reign of Darius the Persian.
verses.bsb: In the days of Eliashib, Joiada, Johanan, and Jaddua, during the reign of Darius the Persian, the heads of the families of the Levites and priests were recorded.

verses.kjv: The sons of Levi, the chief of the fathers, were written in the book of the chronicles, even until the days of Johanan the son of Eliashib.
verses.bsb: As for the descendants of Levi, the family heads up to the days of Johanan son of Eliashib were recorded in the Book of the Chronicles.

verses.kjv: And the chief of the Levites: Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua the son of Kadmiel, with their brethren over against them, to praise and to give thanks, according to the commandment of David the man of God, ward over against ward.
verses.bsb: The leaders of the Levites were Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua son of Kadmiel, along with their associates, who stood across from them to give praise and thanksgiving as one section alternated with the other, as prescribed by David the man of God.

verses.kjv: Mattaniah, and Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, Akkub, were porters keeping the ward at the thresholds of the gates.
verses.bsb: Mattaniah, Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, and Akkub were gatekeepers who guarded the storerooms at the gates.

verses.kjv: These were in the days of Joiakim the son of Jeshua, the son of Jozadak, and in the days of Nehemiah the governor, and of Ezra the priest, the scribe.
verses.bsb: They served in the days of Joiakim son of Jeshua, the son of Jozadak, and in the days of Nehemiah the governor and Ezra the priest and scribe.

verses.kjv: ¶ And at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem they sought the Levites out of all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem, to keep the dedication with gladness, both with thanksgivings, and with singing, with cymbals, psalteries, and with harps.
verses.bsb: At the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem, the Levites were sought out from all their homes and brought to Jerusalem to celebrate the joyous dedication with thanksgiving and singing, accompanied by cymbals, harps, and lyres.

verses.kjv: And the sons of the singers gathered themselves together, both out of the plain country round about Jerusalem, and from the villages of Netophathi;
verses.bsb: The singers were also assembled from the region around Jerusalem, from the villages of the Netophathites,

verses.kjv: Also from the house of Gilgal, and out of the fields of Geba and Azmaveth: for the singers had builded them villages round about Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: from Beth-gilgal, and from the fields of Geba and Azmaveth, for they had built villages for themselves around Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And the priests and the Levites purified themselves, and purified the people, and the gates, and the wall.
verses.bsb: After the priests and Levites had purified themselves, they purified the people, the gates, and the wall.

verses.kjv: Then I brought up the princes of Judah upon the wall, and appointed two great companies of them that gave thanks, whereof one went on the right hand upon the wall toward the dung gate:
verses.bsb: Then I brought the leaders of Judah up on the wall, and I appointed two great thanksgiving choirs. One was to proceed along the top of the wall to the right, toward the Dung Gate.

verses.kjv: And after them went Hoshaiah, and half of the princes of Judah,
verses.bsb: Hoshaiah and half the leaders of Judah followed,

verses.kjv: And Azariah, Ezra, and Meshullam,
verses.bsb: along with Azariah, Ezra, Meshullam,

verses.kjv: Judah, and Benjamin, and Shemaiah, and Jeremiah,
verses.bsb: Judah, Benjamin, Shemaiah, Jeremiah,

verses.kjv: And certain of the priests’ sons with trumpets; namely, Zechariah the son of Jonathan, the son of Shemaiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Michaiah, the son of Zaccur, the son of Asaph:
verses.bsb: and some of the priests with trumpets, and also Zechariah son of Jonathan, the son of Shemaiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Micaiah, the son of Zaccur, the son of Asaph,

verses.kjv: And his brethren, Shemaiah, and Azarael, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethaneel, and Judah, Hanani, with the musical instruments of David the man of God, and Ezra the scribe before them.
verses.bsb: and his associates—Shemaiah, Azarel, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanel, Judah, and Hanani—with the musical instruments prescribed by David the man of God. Ezra the scribe led the procession.

verses.kjv: And at the fountain gate, which was over against them, they went up by the stairs of the city of David, at the going up of the wall, above the house of David, even unto the water gate eastward.
verses.bsb: At the Fountain Gate they climbed the steps of the City of David on the ascent to the wall and passed above the house of David to the Water Gate on the east.

verses.kjv: And the other company of them that gave thanks went over against them, and I after them, and the half of the people upon the wall, from beyond the tower of the furnaces even unto the broad wall;
verses.bsb: The second thanksgiving choir proceeded to the left, and I followed it with half the people along the top of the wall, past the Tower of the Ovens to the Broad Wall,

verses.kjv: And from above the gate of Ephraim, and above the old gate, and above the fish gate, and the tower of Hananeel, and the tower of Meah, even unto the sheep gate: and they stood still in the prison gate.
verses.bsb: over the Gate of Ephraim, the Jeshanah Gate, the Fish Gate, the Tower of Hananel, and the Tower of the Hundred, as far as the Sheep Gate. And they stopped at the Gate of the Guard.

verses.kjv: So stood the two companies of them that gave thanks in the house of God, and I, and the half of the rulers with me:
verses.bsb: The two thanksgiving choirs then stood in the house of God, as did I, along with the half of the officials accompanying me,

verses.kjv: And the priests; Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Michaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah, with trumpets;
verses.bsb: as well as the priests with their trumpets—Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Micaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah—

verses.kjv: And Maaseiah, and Shemaiah, and Eleazar, and Uzzi, and Jehohanan, and Malchijah, and Elam, and Ezer. And the singers sang loud, with Jezrahiah their overseer.
verses.bsb: and also Maaseiah, Shemaiah, Eleazar, Uzzi, Jehohanan, Malchijah, Elam, and Ezer. Then the choirs sang out under the direction of Jezrahiah.

verses.kjv: Also that day they offered great sacrifices, and rejoiced: for God had made them rejoice with great joy: the wives also and the children rejoiced: so that the joy of Jerusalem was heard even afar off.
verses.bsb: On that day they offered great sacrifices, rejoicing because God had given them great joy. The women and children also rejoiced, so that the joy of Jerusalem was heard from afar.

verses.kjv: ¶ And at that time were some appointed over the chambers for the treasures, for the offerings, for the firstfruits, and for the tithes, to gather into them out of the fields of the cities the portions of the law for the priests and Levites: for Judah rejoiced for the priests and for the Levites that waited.
verses.bsb: And on that same day men were appointed over the rooms that housed the supplies, contributions, firstfruits, and tithes. The portions specified by the Law for the priests and Levites were gathered into these storerooms from the fields of the villages, because Judah rejoiced over the priests and Levites who were serving.

verses.kjv: And both the singers and the porters kept the ward of their God, and the ward of the purification, according to the commandment of David, and of Solomon his son.
verses.bsb: They performed the service of their God and the service of purification, along with the singers and gatekeepers, as David and his son Solomon had prescribed.

verses.kjv: For in the days of David and Asaph of old there were chief of the singers, and songs of praise and thanksgiving unto God.
verses.bsb: For long ago, in the days of David and Asaph, there were directors for the singers and for the songs of praise and thanksgiving to God.

verses.kjv: And all Israel in the days of Zerubbabel, and in the days of Nehemiah, gave the portions of the singers and the porters, every day his portion: and they sanctified holy things unto the Levites; and the Levites sanctified them unto the children of Aaron.
verses.bsb: So in the days of Zerubbabel and Nehemiah, all Israel contributed the daily portions for the singers and gatekeepers. They also set aside daily portions for the Levites, and the Levites set aside daily portions for the descendants of Aaron.

verses.kjv: ON that day they read in the book of Moses in the audience of the people; and therein was found written, that the Ammonite and the Moabite should not come into the congregation of God for ever;
verses.bsb: At that time the Book of Moses was read aloud in the hearing of the people, and in it they found the passage stating that no Ammonite or Moabite should ever enter the assembly of God,

verses.kjv: Because they met not the children of Israel with bread and with water, but hired Balaam against them, that he should curse them: howbeit our God turned the curse into a blessing.
verses.bsb: because they had not met the Israelites with food and water, but had hired Balaam to call down a curse against them (although our God had turned the curse into a blessing).

verses.kjv: Now it came to pass, when they had heard the law, that they separated from Israel all the mixed multitude.
verses.bsb: As soon as the people heard this law, they excluded from Israel all of foreign descent.

verses.kjv: ¶ And before this, Eliashib the priest, having the oversight of the chamber of the house of our God, was allied unto Tobiah:
verses.bsb: Now before this, Eliashib the priest, a relative of Tobiah, had been put in charge of the storerooms of the house of our God

verses.kjv: And he had prepared for him a great chamber, where aforetime they laid the meat offerings, the frankincense, and the vessels, and the tithes of the corn, the new wine, and the oil, which was commanded to be given to the Levites, and the singers, and the porters; and the offerings of the priests.
verses.bsb: and had prepared for Tobiah a large room where they had previously stored the grain offerings, the frankincense, the temple articles, and the tithes of grain, new wine, and oil prescribed for the Levites, singers, and gatekeepers, along with the contributions for the priests.

verses.kjv: But in all this time was not I at Jerusalem: for in the two and thirtieth year of Artaxerxes king of Babylon came I unto the king, and after certain days obtained I leave of the king:
verses.bsb: While all this was happening, I was not in Jerusalem, because I had returned to Artaxerxes king of Babylon in the thirty-second year of his reign. Some time later I obtained leave from the king

verses.kjv: And I came to Jerusalem, and understood of the evil that Eliashib did for Tobiah, in preparing him a chamber in the courts of the house of God.
verses.bsb: to return to Jerusalem. Then I discovered the evil that Eliashib had done on behalf of Tobiah by providing him a room in the courts of the house of God.

verses.kjv: And it grieved me sore: therefore I cast forth all the household stuff of Tobiah out of the chamber.
verses.bsb: And I was greatly displeased and threw all of Tobiah’s household goods out of the room.

verses.kjv: Then I commanded, and they cleansed the chambers: and thither brought I again the vessels of the house of God, with the meat offering and the frankincense.
verses.bsb: Then I ordered that the rooms be purified, and I had the articles of the house of God restored to them, along with the grain offerings and frankincense.

verses.kjv: ¶ And I perceived that the portions of the Levites had not been given them: for the Levites and the singers, that did the work, were fled every one to his field.
verses.bsb: I also learned that because the portions for the Levites had not been given to them, all the Levites and singers responsible for performing the service had gone back to their own fields.

verses.kjv: Then contended I with the rulers, and said, Why is the house of God forsaken? And I gathered them together, and set them in their place.
verses.bsb: So I rebuked the officials and asked, “Why has the house of God been neglected?”  Then I gathered the Levites and singers together and stationed them at their posts,

verses.kjv: Then brought all Judah the tithe of the corn and the new wine and the oil unto the treasuries.
verses.bsb: and all Judah brought a tenth of the grain, new wine, and oil into the storerooms.

verses.kjv: And I made treasurers over the treasuries, Shelemiah the priest, and Zadok the scribe, and of the Levites, Pedaiah: and next to them was Hanan the son of Zaccur, the son of Mattaniah: for they were counted faithful, and their office was to distribute unto their brethren.
verses.bsb: I appointed as treasurers over the storerooms Shelemiah the priest, Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah of the Levites, with Hanan son of Zaccur, the son of Mattaniah, to assist them, because they were considered trustworthy. They were responsible for distributing the supplies to their fellow Levites.

verses.kjv: Remember me, O my God, concerning this, and wipe not out my good deeds that I have done for the house of my God, and for the offices thereof.
verses.bsb: Remember me for this, O my God, and do not blot out my deeds of loving devotion for the house of my God and for its services.

verses.kjv: ¶ In those days saw I in Judah some treading wine presses on the sabbath, and bringing in sheaves, and lading asses; as also wine, grapes, and figs, and all manner of burdens, which they brought into Jerusalem on the sabbath day: and I testified against them in the day wherein they sold victuals.
verses.bsb: In those days I saw people in Judah treading winepresses on the Sabbath and bringing in grain and loading it on donkeys, along with wine, grapes, and figs. All kinds of goods were being brought into Jerusalem on the Sabbath day. So I warned them against selling food on that day.

verses.kjv: There dwelt men of Tyre also therein, which brought fish, and all manner of ware, and sold on the sabbath unto the children of Judah, and in Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Additionally, men of Tyre who lived there were importing fish and all kinds of merchandise and selling them on the Sabbath to the people of Judah in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: Then I contended with the nobles of Judah, and said unto them, What evil thing is this that ye do, and profane the sabbath day?
verses.bsb: Then I rebuked the nobles of Judah and asked, “What is this evil you are doing—profaning the Sabbath day?

verses.kjv: Did not your fathers thus, and did not our God bring all this evil upon us, and upon this city? yet ye bring more wrath upon Israel by profaning the sabbath.
verses.bsb: Did not your forefathers do the same things, so that our God brought all this disaster on us and on this city? And now you are rekindling His wrath against Israel by profaning the Sabbath!”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that when the gates of Jerusalem began to be dark before the sabbath, I commanded that the gates should be shut, and charged that they should not be opened till after the sabbath: and some of my servants set I at the gates, that there should no burden be brought in on the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: When the evening shadows began to fall on the gates of Jerusalem, just before the Sabbath, I ordered that the gates be shut and not opened until after the Sabbath. I posted some of my servants at the gates so that no load could enter on the Sabbath day.

verses.kjv: So the merchants and sellers of all kind of ware lodged without Jerusalem once or twice.
verses.bsb: Once or twice, the merchants and those who sell all kinds of goods camped outside Jerusalem,

verses.kjv: Then I testified against them, and said unto them, Why lodge ye about the wall? if ye do so again, I will lay hands on you. From that time forth came they no more on the sabbath.
verses.bsb: but I warned them, “Why are you camping in front of the wall? If you do it again, I will lay hands on you.” From that time on, they did not return on the Sabbath.

verses.kjv: And I commanded the Levites that they should cleanse themselves, and that they should come and keep the gates, to sanctify the sabbath day. Remember me, O my God, concerning this also, and spare me according to the greatness of thy mercy.
verses.bsb: Then I instructed the Levites to purify themselves and guard the gates in order to keep the Sabbath day holy.  Remember me for this as well, O my God, and show me mercy according to Your abundant loving devotion.

verses.kjv: ¶ In those days also saw I Jews that had married wives of Ashdod, of Ammon, and of Moab:
verses.bsb: In those days I also saw Jews who had married women from Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab.

verses.kjv: And their children spake half in the speech of Ashdod, and could not speak in the Jews’ language, but according to the language of each people.
verses.bsb: Half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod or of the other peoples, but could not speak the language of Judah.

verses.kjv: And I contended with them, and cursed them, and smote certain of them, and plucked off their hair, and made them swear by God, saying, Ye shall not give your daughters unto their sons, nor take their daughters unto your sons, or for yourselves.
verses.bsb: I rebuked them and called down curses on them. I beat some of these men and pulled out their hair.  Then I made them take an oath before God and said, “You must not give your daughters in marriage to their sons or take their daughters as wives for your sons or for yourselves!

verses.kjv: Did not Solomon king of Israel sin by these things? yet among many nations was there no king like him, who was beloved of his God, and God made him king over all Israel: nevertheless even him did outlandish women cause to sin.
verses.bsb: Did not King Solomon of Israel sin in matters like this? There was not a king like him among many nations, and he was loved by his God, who made him king over all Israel—yet foreign women drew him into sin.

verses.kjv: Shall we then hearken unto you to do all this great evil, to transgress against our God in marrying strange wives?
verses.bsb: Must we now hear that you too are doing all this terrible evil and acting unfaithfully against our God by marrying foreign women?”

verses.kjv: And one of the sons of Joiada, the son of Eliashib the high priest, was son in law to Sanballat the Horonite: therefore I chased him from me.
verses.bsb: Even one of the sons of Jehoiada son of Eliashib the high priest had become a son-in-law to Sanballat the Horonite. Therefore I drove him away from me.

verses.kjv: Remember them, O my God, because they have defiled the priesthood, and the covenant of the priesthood, and of the Levites.
verses.bsb: Remember them, O my God, because they have defiled the priesthood and the covenant of the priesthood and of the Levites.

verses.kjv: Thus cleansed I them from all strangers, and appointed the wards of the priests and the Levites, every one in his business;
verses.bsb: Thus I purified the priests and Levites from everything foreign, and I assigned specific duties to each of the priests and Levites.

verses.kjv: And for the wood offering, at times appointed, and for the firstfruits. Remember me, O my God, for good. THE
verses.bsb: I also arranged for contributions of wood at the appointed times, and for the firstfruits.  Remember me, O my God, with favor.
